query_result.reasoning: Now let me also retrieve information about the specific Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation and non-GAAP measures to understand the full picture of how the recast affects key metrics.

tool_calls.name: retrieve_information
tool_calls.args: {"prompt": "Retrieve the Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table and definition from {{spacex_s1}}, including the starting GAAP metric, each adjustment item, and any non-GAAP measure definitions. Also retrieve any disclosure about related-party transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X that were eliminated in consolidation."}

parsed_args.prompt: Retrieve the Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table and definition from {{spacex_s1}}, including the starting GAAP metric, each adjustment item, and any non-GAAP measure definitions. Also retrieve any disclosure about related-party transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X that were eliminated in consolidation.

tool_output.output:
Based on the provided text, here are the requested items:

### **1. Adjusted EBITDA and Segment Adjusted EBITDA: Definition & Reconciliation**

**Definitions (from the Preamble):**
*   **Adjusted EBITDA** is defined as net income (loss) excluding (i) depreciation and amortization, (ii) share-based compensation, (iii) impairment, (iv) restructuring charges, (v) interest expense, (vi) interest income, (vii) other income (expense), net, and (viii) provision for income taxes.
*   **Segment Adjusted EBITDA** is defined as segment income (loss) from operations excluding (i) depreciation and amortization, (ii) share-based compensation, (iii) restructuring charges, and (iv) impairment.

**Role as Performance Measures (from the Preamble):**
Adjusted EBITDA and Segment Adjusted EBITDA are key performance measures that management uses to assess financial performance, for internal planning, and for forecasting. They are considered meaningful performance measures for investors to evaluate operating performance and compare financial results.

**Reconciliation Table (Consolidated - Net Income to Adjusted EBITDA):**
| (in millions) | Three Months Ended March 31, 2026 | Three Months Ended March 31, 2025 | Year Ended December 31, 2025 | Year Ended December 31, 2024 | Year Ended December 31, 2023 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Net income (loss)** | **$(4,276)** | **$(528)** | **$(4,937)** | **$791** | **$(4,628)** |
| **Add (deduct):** | | | | | |
| Depreciation and amortization | 2,442 | 1,443 | 6,701 | 3,824 | 2,635 |
| Share-based compensation | 639 | 232 | 1,947 | 784 | 679 |
| Restructuring charges | (11) | 4 | 487 | 213 | 237 |
| Impairments | — | 24 | 38 | 63 | 3,775 |
| Interest expense | 664 | 447 | 1,945 | 1,580 | 1,693 |
| Interest income | (213) | (117) | (492) | (371) | (249) |
| Other (income) expense, net | 1,876 | 211 | 177 | (985) | 42 |
| Provision for (benefit from) income taxes | 6 | 14 | 718 | (549) | (363) |
| **Adjusted EBITDA** | **$1,127** | **$1,730** | **$6,584** | **$5,350** | **$3,821** |

**Reconciliation Tables (Segment - Segment Income/Loss from Operations to Segment Adjusted EBITDA):**
*Tables for Q1 2026, Q1 2025, FY 2025, and FY 2024 are provided in the source text, showing adjustments for Depreciation & Amortization, Share-based Compensation, Restructuring Charges, and Impairments for the Space, Connectivity, and AI segments.*

---

### **2. Related-Party Transactions Eliminated in Consolidation**

The following transactions between SpaceX, xAI, X, and other entities affiliated with Elon Musk were identified as related-party transactions and have been eliminated in the consolidated financial statements.

**Transactions with Tesla (SpaceX & xAI):**
*   **SpaceX commercial agreements:** Obtained goods/services of $11M (2023), $4M (2024), $144M (2025), and $0.2M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).
*   **xAI commercial, licensing, and support agreements:** Obtained goods/services of $191M (2024), $506M (2025), and $34M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026). Recognized revenue of $2M (2025) and $0.4M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).
*   **X Holdings advertising agreements:** Tesla purchased advertising of $0.5M (2024), $4M (2025), and $0 (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).
*   **Aircraft usage:** SpaceX charged Tesla $1M (2023), $1M (2024), $2M (2025), and $0 (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).

**Transactions with The Boring Company (X & SpaceX):**
*   **X Office Space Lease:** X made lease payments of $0.1M (2024), $1M (2025), and $0.1M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).
*   **SpaceX Tunnel Construction:** SpaceX incurred $1M in expenses (2025) for tunnel construction in Bastrop, TX.

**Transactions with Musk Industries LLC (xAI):**
*   **xAI Real Property Lease:** xAI made lease payments of $0.5M (2024), $2M (2025), and $0.2M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).

**Transactions with Musk-owned Security Company (SpaceX):**
*   **Security Services:** SpaceX incurred expenses of $2M (2023), $3M (2024), $4M (2025), and $1M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).

**Transactions with Valor Equity Partners (xAI, affiliated with board member Antonio J. Gracias):**
*   **Equipment Lease Agreements:** Subsidiaries of xAI have three major equipment lease agreements with Valor for computing hardware, requiring aggregate cash payments of $6,986M, $6,633M, and $6,587M over the lease terms. SpaceX or its subsidiaries guarantee these obligations. Payments made were $885M (2025) and $857M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).
*   **API Services:** X received payments from Valor of $1M (2024), $1M (2025), and $0.1M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).

**Accounting Treatment (from Financial Notes):**
The mergers of xAI and X into SpaceX were accounted for as reorganizations under common control because Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest in all entities. Consequently, all historical related-party transactions between the entities **have been eliminated in consolidation** for all periods presented.
